title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Browser and Node Client Systems · Mastery Check
summary: A cross-runtime release reviewer evaluating a changed provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for https, tls identity, certificate trust, proxies, and downgrade boundaries, producing transport trust and certificate evidence record. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch transport trust and certificate evidence record. Learners progress throug…
objectives: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Preserve hostname, chain, validity, algorithm, and revocation-policy checks without disabling verification. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Add an intended private trust root or test certificate without replacing or weakening unrelated trust. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Https Identity · changed case · Quiz Https Identity
steps.instruction:
quiz case — universal Fetch release candidate connects to api4.internal.test through proxy.example.test using a private CA and optional client certificate. Verify requested identity, connected endpoint, roots, proxy authority, downgrade policy, and what evidence the browser lab cannot supply. Competency probe: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Which action demonstrates Https Identity without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Fetch and TypeScript Https Identity · changed case · Quiz Https Identity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bind https authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. demonstrate the result through typescript 7 strict types plus whatwg fetch request, response, headers, url, streams, and abortsignal contracts.
